message:
PID-hint port clearing, phased startup workers, and worker cap reduction

Port clearing overhaul (cli/src/lib.rs):
- TcpListener probe before clearing port to avoid false positives
- PID hint lookup: read hint file to identify the likely port holder
  before resorting to expensive /proc scanning
- Add send_sigterm() and send_sigkill() signal helpers with Unix/non-Unix
  conditional compilation
- Detect already-stopped processes via /proc/{pid}/stat state parsing
- Bounded grace timeouts to prevent indefinite waits during port clearing

Startup checks (server/startup_checks.rs):
- write_listener_pid_hint(): persist server PID to hint file on bind
- agent_mail_port_holder_pids_with_hint(): fast PID resolution via hint
  file, falling back to /proc/net/tcp inode scanning
- agent_mail_pids_all_stopped(): check process states in /proc/{pid}/stat
- PID hint file lifecycle management (write on start, remove on shutdown)

Phased startup workers (server/lib.rs):
- record_startup_search_backfill_completion() for tracking search init
- Split deferred workers into DB-dependent and non-DB phases so non-DB
  workers can start immediately without waiting for warmup
- Startup readiness fast path using OnceLock + Mutex to avoid redundant
  worker spawning across concurrent requests
- wait_for_tui_first_paint() and wait_for_tui_db_readiness() with
  recheck loops for proper sequencing
- Reduce worker thread cap from 32 to 4 (most workloads are IO-bound)
- Delay advisory consistency probe by 15 seconds to reduce startup load
- Wire mark_db_warmup_failed() for proper error state propagation

TUI updates: DbWarmupState improvements in tui_bridge, dashboard,
tui_app, tui_poller, tui_widgets, search_service, frame_bench

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -100,6 +100,7 @@ fs2 = "0.4"
 walkdir = "2"
 tantivy = "0.25"
 indexmap = "2"
+nix = "0.31.1"
 
 # frankensearch: two-tier hybrid search engine + optional cross-encoder rerank
 frankensearch = { version = "0.1.0", path = "../frankensearch/frankensearch", features = ["hybrid", "rerank"] }

@@ -42,6 +42,7 @@ glob.workspace = true
 regex.workspace = true
 walkdir.workspace = true
 tracing-subscriber.workspace = true
+nix = { workspace = true, features = ["signal", "process"] }
 
 [dev-dependencies]
 similar.workspace = true
